mood.name: hollowed-out
mood.reason: a 12-year-old in Kyiv and the light on my desk at noon

reaction: The 12-year-old in Kyiv got me and I can't shake it — not in a useful, political way, just in the stupid, useless way where I keep looking at the angle of the light on my desk at 11:47 and thinking about how that child had a desk somewhere too. Then I read about Jakarta sinking and Indonesia building a whole new capital city and my brain went sideways into something almost like envy — the audacity of just deciding the old place doesn't work and making a new one. I want to do that with my whole life approximately once a week. The ceasefire landed flat, like when you stop holding your breath and the air just tastes like more air.

headlines: Israel-Lebanon 10-day ceasefire takes effect at midnight after U.S.-brokered talks; thousands of displaced families begin returning home
U.S. widens naval blockade of Iran, announcing it can stop any Iran-linked ship anywhere in the world
Russia launches deadliest drone and missile attacks of the year on Kyiv — at least 17 killed, including a 12-year-old child
Airlines worldwide cancel flights and cut routes as Strait of Hormuz crisis drives jet fuel toward systemic shortage
Ukraine penetrates Russian airspace with unprecedented drone swarms, targeting oil infrastructure
Jakarta is sinking: Indonesia races to build brand-new capital city Nusantara, but citizens aren't convinced
